title: Weftmap
hero: Understand any code at a glance.
tagline: Paste code and get an interactive call graph. Multi-language via Tree-sitter.

steps.title: Paste your code
steps.desc: Pick a language and drop in a snippet — no setup, no upload.

steps.title: Tree-sitter parses it
steps.desc: A real syntax tree is built on the server, fast and language-agnostic.

steps.title: Explore the call graph
steps.desc: Pan, zoom and follow how functions call each other.

features.title: Multi-language
features.desc: One parsing engine, many languages. Powered by Tree-sitter.

features.title: Interactive diagrams
features.desc: Pan, zoom and explore the call graph of your code.

features.title: Pluggable
features.desc: Add a new language in just a few lines. Built to extend.

features.title: Accurate parsing
features.desc: Real syntax trees via Tree-sitter, not fragile regex.

features.title: No setup
features.desc: Paste a snippet and visualize. Nothing to install.

footerNote: Open source, built to be extended.
noFunctions: No functions found in the code.
noTables: No tables found in the SQL.
projectHint: Pick a folder — its files are mapped as one project graph.
showcaseCallDesc: Functions become nodes and calls become arrows — traced across files.
showcaseSchemaDesc: Tables become entities and foreign keys become relationships with cardinality.
languagesSubtitle: Six today — five produce a call graph, SQL produces an ER diagram.

languageRows.name: JavaScript
languageRows.detail: Functions, arrow fns, classes, ES imports

languageRows.name: TypeScript
languageRows.detail: Same as JS, with the typed grammar

useCases.title: Onboard to a new codebase
useCases.desc: Get the lay of the land before reading line by line.

useCases.title: Review changes faster
useCases.desc: See how a function connects before approving a PR.

useCases.title: Teach and document
useCases.desc: Turn code into a diagram for explanations and docs.

useCases.title: Explore unfamiliar code
useCases.desc: Drop in a snippet or file and trace the flow.

faqs.q: Does my code leave my machine?
faqs.a: It's sent to the server to be parsed with Tree-sitter, then discarded — nothing is stored.
Up to 2 MB and 400 files per analysis.
Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Go, Rust and SQL — with more on the way.

faqs.q: Is it free and open source?
faqs.a: Yes, MIT-licensed. Contributions — especially new languages — are welcome.

ctaDesc: Paste a snippet and get an interactive diagram in seconds.
ctaOssDesc: Built to be extended. Star it or pick up a good first issue.
notFoundTitle: This page got lost in the graph
notFoundDesc: The link may be broken or the page may have moved. Let's get you back on track.
